# Template Registry

The Template Registry is the system that manages tenant-specific print templates for LIMS report generation.

## Overview

Every report generated by the platform is rendered using a **PrintTemplate**. Templates are tenant-owned, versioned configurations that control the visual structure of reports without altering the document generation pipeline or workflow state machine.

## Core Model

### TemplateBlueprint (System-owned)

Platform-level starter template definitions. These are immutable reference definitions that tenants copy from during onboarding or provisioning. No tenant owns a blueprint directly.

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `id` | UUID |
| `code` | Globally unique code (e.g. `general_table_v1`) |
| `name` | Human-readable name |
| `templateFamily` | Renderer family (e.g. `GENERAL_TABLE`) |
| `schemaType` | Compatible result schema type (e.g. `TABULAR`) |
| `defaultConfigJson` | Initial config JSON for this family |
| `isActive` | Whether available for provisioning |
| `sortOrder` | Display order in provisioning UI |

### PrintTemplate (Tenant-owned)

Working template owned by a specific tenant. Created by cloning a blueprint, cloning an existing template, or creating from a family shell.

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `id` | UUID |
| `tenantId` | Owner tenant (required) |
| `sourceBlueprintId` | Blueprint this was provisioned from (nullable) |
| `code` | Tenant-scoped code |
| `name` | Human-readable name |
| `schemaType` | Compatible result schema type |
| `templateFamily` | Renderer family |
| `templateVersion` | Integer version (starts at 1) |
| `status` | `DRAFT` / `ACTIVE` / `ARCHIVED` |
| `configJson` | Structured layout/display config |
| `isSystemProvisioned` | Was this provisioned automatically during onboarding? |
| `createdByUserId` | Admin user who created this (nullable) |
| `supersedesTemplateId` | If this is a new version, the ID of the template it succeeds |

**Unique constraint**: `(tenantId, code, templateVersion)` — multiple versions of the same code are allowed.

### TestTemplateMap (Tenant-scoped mapping)

Many-to-many mapping between `CatalogTest` and `PrintTemplate`. Controls which templates are available for a test and which is the default.

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `id` | UUID |
| `tenantId` | Owner tenant |
| `testId` | CatalogTest ID |
| `templateId` | PrintTemplate ID |
| `isDefault` | Is this the default template for the test? |
| `sortOrder` | Display order when multiple templates are available |
| `isEnabled` | Is this mapping active? |

**Rules:**
- Only one `isDefault = true` mapping per test at any time.
- Template `schemaType` must match test `resultSchemaType`.
- Template must not be `ARCHIVED`.
- Template must belong to the same tenant as the test.

### CatalogTest Extensions

Two fields added to `CatalogTest`:

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `resultSchemaType` | `TABULAR` | Which result schema this test produces |
| `allowTemplateOverride` | `false` | Whether operator can choose a non-default template at publish time |

## Enums

### TemplateFamily

Controls the renderer used for PDF generation.

| Value | Phase | Description |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| `GENERAL_TABLE` | ✅ Implemented | Standard tabular report |
| `TWO_COLUMN_TABLE` | ✅ Implemented | Two-column compact layout |
| `PERIPHERAL_FILM_REPORT` | 🔲 Placeholder | Hematology descriptive |
| `HISTOPATH_NARRATIVE` | 🔲 Placeholder | Histopathology report |
| `GRAPHICAL_SCALE_REPORT` | 🔲 Placeholder | Graph-based reports (lipid, etc.) |
| `IMAGE_REPORT` | 🔲 Placeholder | Image attachment reports |

### ResultSchemaType

Describes the data structure of test results.

| Value | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `TABULAR` | Standard parameter/value table |
| `DESCRIPTIVE_HEMATOLOGY` | Hematology descriptive narrative |
| `HISTOPATHOLOGY` | Pathology narrative |
| `GRAPH_SERIES` | Time-series or range graph data |
| `IMAGE_ATTACHMENT` | Image file attachment |
| `MIXED_STRUCTURED` | Mixed content types |

### TemplateStatus

| Value | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `DRAFT` | Under construction, not used for real documents |
| `ACTIVE` | In use — the live version for real documents |
| `ARCHIVED` | Retired, kept for historical document reference only |

## Family → Schema Compatibility Matrix

| Family | Allowed Schema Types |
|--------|---------------------|
| `GENERAL_TABLE` | `TABULAR` |
| `TWO_COLUMN_TABLE` | `TABULAR` |
| `PERIPHERAL_FILM_REPORT` | `DESCRIPTIVE_HEMATOLOGY` |
| `HISTOPATH_NARRATIVE` | `HISTOPATHOLOGY` |
| `GRAPHICAL_SCALE_REPORT` | `GRAPH_SERIES` |
| `IMAGE_REPORT` | `IMAGE_ATTACHMENT` |

## Document Pipeline Integration

When `generateFromEncounter()` runs:

1. `resolveReportTemplate(tenantId, testId)` is called.
2. If a `TestTemplateMap` default exists for the test, that template is used.
3. Otherwise, the tenant's active `GENERAL_TABLE` template is used.
4. If no template is configured at all, returns `null` (backward-compatible legacy behavior).
5. Template identity fields (`templateCode`, `templateVersion`, `templateFamily`) are injected into the LAB_REPORT canonical payload.
6. These fields are included in the `payloadHash`, so documents are deterministically tied to the template version.

**Historical documents are not affected** — they were generated before the template system and have no template fields in their payload hash.

## API Endpoints

All template endpoints require the `templates.read` or `templates.write` permission.

| Method | Path | Permission | Description |
|--------|------|-----------|-------------|
| GET | `/admin/template-blueprints` | `templates.read` | List all active blueprints |
| POST | `/admin/template-blueprints/provision-defaults` | `templates.provision` | Provision blueprints into tenant templates |
| GET | `/admin/templates` | `templates.read` | List tenant templates |
| POST | `/admin/templates` | `templates.write` | Create a new template |
| GET | `/admin/templates/{id}` | `templates.read` | Get a template |
| PATCH | `/admin/templates/{id}` | `templates.write` | Update (creates new draft if ACTIVE) |
| POST | `/admin/templates/{id}/clone` | `templates.write` | Clone a template |
| POST | `/admin/templates/{id}/new-version` | `templates.write` | Create new draft version |
| POST | `/admin/templates/{id}/activate` | `templates.write` | Activate a draft |
| POST | `/admin/templates/{id}/archive` | `templates.write` | Archive a template |
| POST | `/admin/templates/{id}/preview` | `templates.read` | Generate preview PDF |
| GET | `/admin/catalog/tests/{testId}/templates` | `templates.read` | Get test template mappings |
| PUT | `/admin/catalog/tests/{testId}/templates` | `templates.write` | Set test template mappings |

## Future Extension

To add a new renderer family:
1. Add the value to `TEMPLATE_FAMILIES` in `templates-validation.ts`
2. Add its schema compatibility entry to `FAMILY_SCHEMA_COMPATIBILITY`
3. Implement the renderer in the PDF service
4. Add a blueprint for the new family in the seed file
5. No schema migration required — the family and schema type fields are strings

See [TEMPLATE_COMPATIBILITY_RULES.md](./TEMPLATE_COMPATIBILITY_RULES.md) for full validation rules.
